Past history, medications, baseline, and recent change all affect interpretation.
Endocrine problems are often systemic metabolic disturbances, not local complaints.
Assessment must combine symptoms, history, numbers, and the overall appearance.
mustKnowZh: 看到“整体不对劲”的病人时，要记得 endocrine differential。
要重视 objective data。
要把既往病史和当前表现一起想。
mustKnowEn: When a patient looks “globally wrong,” keep endocrine causes in your differential.
Take objective data seriously.
Think about past history together with the current presentation.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：觉得 endocrine emergency 一定很典型。实际上常常很模糊。
常见误区 2：只看一个 symptom，不看 systemic pattern。
常见误区 3：忽略 blood glucose / medication history。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: expecting endocrine emergencies to look obvious. In reality, they are often vague.
Common confusion 2: focusing on one symptom instead of the systemic pattern.
Common confusion 3: ignoring blood glucose or medication history.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 allergic reactions，重点在识别过敏反应何时升级成 anaphylaxis，以及 airway / breathing / circulation 何时开始被牵连。它真正训练的是：不要被皮肤表现迷惑。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers allergic reactions, focusing on recognizing when an allergic response progresses into anaphylaxis and when airway, breathing, and circulation become involved. Its deeper lesson is not to be distracted by skin findings alone.
highlightsZh: 不要只看皮疹
airway / breathing / circulation involvement 才是高危信号
过敏反应可能短时间升级
highlightsEn: Do not look only at the rash
Airway, breathing, and circulation involvement are the real danger signals
Allergic reactions may escalate quickly
keyPointsZh: 过敏反应最危险的部分不一定在皮肤，而在系统性受累。
声音改变、呼吸困难、喘鸣、循环不稳都提示病情升级。
mild allergy 和 anaphylaxis 的临床意义完全不同。
过敏病人可能在短时间内明显恶化，因此 reassessment 非常重要。
airway thinking 在这章里始终非常靠前。
keyPointsEn: The most dangerous part of an allergic reaction is often not the skin, but systemic involvement.
Voice change, dyspnea, wheeze, and circulatory instability all suggest escalation.
Mild allergy and anaphylaxis have very different clinical meanings.
Allergic patients may deteriorate significantly in a short time, so reassessment is critical.
Airway thinking remains near the top of priorities throughout this chapter.
mustKnowZh: 不要只看有没有荨麻疹。
要把 airway / breathing / circulation 一起评估。
要记住 allergic reaction 是动态的。
mustKnowEn: Do not judge the problem only by the presence or absence of hives.
Assess airway, breathing, and circulation together.
Remember that allergic reactions are dynamic.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 allergy 只看成皮肤问题。
常见误区 2：觉得病人现在还能说话就暂时不危险。
常见误区 3：一次 assessment 后就不再重看。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating allergy as only a skin problem.
Common confusion 2: assuming that because the patient can still talk, the situation is not yet dangerous.
Common confusion 3: performing one assessment and failing to reassess.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 gastrointestinal emergencies，重点在腹痛、呕吐、腹泻等常见 complaint 背后的不同严重度和危险机制。它真正训练的是：GI complaint 很常见，但你不能因为常见就低估。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers gastrointestinal emergencies, focusing on the different severities and dangerous mechanisms behind common complaints such as abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea. At its core, it teaches that common does not mean low risk.
highlightsZh: GI complaint 很常见，但病因跨度很大
要警惕 dehydration、bleeding、infection 和 surgical abdomen risk
general appearance 会改变风险判断
highlightsEn: GI complaints are common, but causes range widely in severity
Watch for dehydration, bleeding, infection, and surgical-abdomen risk
General appearance changes the level of concern
keyPointsZh: GI complaints 不能只靠主诉名称判断严重度。
腹痛、恶心、呕吐和腹泻都可能是从轻症到重症的不同入口。
overall appearance、perfusion、pain pattern 和 trend 很重要。
一些 GI complaint 的危险在于脱水、出血、感染或急腹症。
assessment 要帮助你区分“很不舒服”和“很危险”这两件事。
keyPointsEn: You cannot determine GI severity from the complaint label alone.
Abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea may all represent anything from minor illness to major emergency.
Overall appearance, perfusion, pain pattern, and trend matter greatly.
Some GI complaints are dangerous because of dehydration, bleeding, infection, or a surgical abdomen.
Assessment should help you distinguish “very uncomfortable” from “truly dangerous.”
mustKnowZh: 不要把腹痛当成单一种 complaint。
要注意脱水和 perfusion risk。
要用整体状态辅助判断 GI danger level。
mustKnowEn: Do not treat abdominal pain as a single simple complaint.
Pay attention to dehydration and perfusion risk.
Use overall presentation to judge the danger level of GI complaints.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：因为 GI complaint 常见就自动降级风险。
常见误区 2：只看疼痛，不看 overall appearance 和 perfusion。
常见误区 3：把腹泻/呕吐只当不舒服，不看系统性后果。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: automatically downgrading risk because GI complaints are common.
Common confusion 2: focusing only on pain and ignoring overall appearance and perfusion.
Common confusion 3: treating vomiting/diarrhea as discomfort only and missing systemic consequences.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 renal and urologic emergencies，重点在排泄系统问题如何影响疼痛、感染、梗阻与整体生理平衡。它真正训练的是：不要把 renal/urologic complaint 简化成“只是疼”。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers renal and urologic emergencies, focusing on how urinary-system problems affect pain, infection, obstruction, and overall physiology. At its core, it teaches you not to reduce renal/urologic complaints to “just pain.”
highlightsZh: renal complaint 可能是系统性问题
history 很关键
不能只看局部疼痛
highlightsEn: Renal complaints may be systemic problems
History is critical
Do not look only at local pain
keyPointsZh: 肾泌尿 complaint 可能伴随 infection、obstruction、metabolic problem 或 perfusion consequence。
既往结石、感染、慢性肾病和排尿习惯改变都很重要。
病人看起来有多 sick 和 local pain 一样重要。
renal/urologic problem 可能从局部不适快速升级成系统问题。
assessment 应帮助你判断这是 isolated pain 还是 broader physiology problem。
keyPointsEn: Renal/urologic complaints may involve infection, obstruction, metabolic disturbance, or perfusion consequences.
Past stones, infection, chronic kidney disease, and changes in urination are all important history clues.
How sick the patient looks matters as much as the local pain complaint.
Renal/urologic problems may progress from local discomfort to systemic illness.
Assessment should help determine whether this is isolated pain or part of a broader physiologic problem.
mustKnowZh: 不要把 renal/urologic complaint 自动归类成“pain control”。
要看 history。
要判断病人是不是已经 systemic。
mustKnowEn: Do not automatically classify renal/urologic complaints as “pain control” cases.
Look closely at history.
Determine whether the patient is already systemically unwell.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：只看 flank pain，不看 infection / obstruction risk。
常见误区 2：觉得这类问题不会很危险。
常见误区 3：忽略病人的 overall appearance 和 trend。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: focusing only on flank pain and missing infection or obstruction risk.
Common confusion 2: assuming these complaints are never very dangerous.
Common confusion 3: ignoring overall appearance and trend.

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 36 — Toxicology: Substance Abuse and Poisoning
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 36 — Toxicology: Substance Abuse and Poisoning
reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 toxicology: substance abuse and poisoning，重点在毒理学病人 presentation 可以非常多样，而且经常需要把 scene clues、history 和 patient appearance 拼在一起。它真正训练的是 pattern recognition。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers toxicology: substance abuse and poisoning. Its focus is that toxicology presentations can be extremely varied and often require combining scene clues, history, and patient appearance. At its core, it trains pattern recognition.
highlightsZh: tox case 常像拼图
scene clues 很关键
airway / breathing / mentation risk 要一直放前面
highlightsEn: Toxicology cases often feel like puzzles
Scene clues are extremely important
Airway, breathing, and mentation risks must stay at the front of your thinking
keyPointsZh: 中毒和 substance abuse 可以模仿很多其他 emergency。
scene clues（药瓶、气味、环境、旁人信息）常常是高价值线索。
病人真正危险的地方常在 airway、breathing、circulation 或 altered LOC。
单一线索往往不够，毒理 assessment 常常是多线索整合。
这章训练的是 broad differential + pattern recognition。
keyPointsEn: Poisoning and substance abuse can mimic many other emergencies.
Scene clues—pill bottles, odors, environment, bystander information—are often very high-value.
The real danger often lies in airway, breathing, circulation, or altered LOC.
One clue is rarely enough; toxicology assessment usually requires integration of multiple clues.
This chapter trains broad differential thinking plus pattern recognition.
mustKnowZh: 看到“哪都不太对”的病人时，要想到 tox。
要把 scene clue 和 patient presentation 连起来。
要始终把 airway / breathing / mentation 放前面。
mustKnowEn: When a patient looks generally “wrong,” keep toxicology on the list.
Link scene clues to the patient’s presentation.
Always keep airway, breathing, and mentation near the top of priorities.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：只想找一个确定答案。实际上 tox 常常只能靠拼图。
常见误区 2：因为病人清醒就低估风险。
常见误区 3：只看病人，不看 scene clues。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: wanting a single definitive clue immediately. In reality, toxicology is often puzzle-based.
Common confusion 2: underestimating danger because the patient is still awake.
Common confusion 3: looking only at the patient and ignoring the scene clues.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 hematologic emergencies，重点在血液系统问题如何通过 oxygen delivery、coagulation 和 perfusion 影响全身。它真正训练的是：血液问题不是实验室题，而是全身生理题。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers hematologic emergencies, focusing on how blood-system problems affect the whole body through oxygen delivery, coagulation, and perfusion. At its core, it teaches that hematology is not just a lab topic, but a full-body physiology topic.
highlightsZh: 血液系统问题常是 systemic problem
coagulation / perfusion / oxygen delivery 都相关
症状和生命体征要一起看
highlightsEn: Blood-system problems are often systemic problems
Coagulation, perfusion, and oxygen delivery are all involved
Symptoms and vital signs must be interpreted together
keyPointsZh: hematologic emergency 的重点不只是“血液有问题”，而是血液问题会怎么影响器官供氧和整体稳定。
coagulation abnormalities、oxygen-carrying issues 和 perfusion failure 都可能让病人很快恶化。
这类病人 often need whole-patient thinking，而不是单一 complaint thinking。
体征变化、skin signs、mentality 和 perfusion clues 都很有价值。
这章会把你对“血液只是实验室概念”的印象拉回临床现实。
keyPointsEn: The focus of hematologic emergencies is not just that “something is wrong with the blood,” but how that problem affects oxygen delivery and overall stability.
Coagulation abnormalities, oxygen-carrying problems, and perfusion failure can all lead to rapid deterioration.
These patients often require whole-patient thinking rather than single-complaint thinking.
Changes in vital signs, skin findings, mentation, and perfusion all provide high-value information.
This chapter moves hematology out of the lab and back into clinical reality.
mustKnowZh: 要把 blood problem 和 system problem 连起来想。
要知道 coagulation / oxygen delivery / perfusion 会一起影响病人。
要重视整体表现，而不只是表面 complaint。
mustKnowEn: Link blood problems to systems problems.
Know that coagulation, oxygen delivery, and perfusion all interact.
Pay attention to the whole patient, not just the visible complaint.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 hematologic problem 当成只是“医生看实验室”的事。
常见误区 2：觉得只要没有明显出血就不严重。
常见误区 3：把血液系统问题和 perfusion 分开看。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating hematologic problems as something only physicians or lab work deal with.
Common confusion 2: assuming that no obvious bleeding means no major problem.
Common confusion 3: separating blood-system problems from perfusion thinking.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 environmental emergencies，重点在环境暴露本身如何成为病因，影响体温、体液平衡和整体生理状态。它真正训练的是：scene 本身有时就是 diagnosis 的一部分。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers environmental emergencies, focusing on how environmental exposure itself becomes the cause of illness, affecting temperature, fluid balance, and overall physiology. At its core, it teaches that the scene itself may be part of the diagnosis.
highlightsZh: scene 与 exposure history 很重要
要先切断持续暴露
core temperature / hydration / perfusion 都相关
highlightsEn: Scene and exposure history are highly important
Ongoing exposure must be stopped first
Core temperature, hydration, and perfusion are closely linked
keyPointsZh: 环境急症的病因经常就写在暴露史里。
热、冷、水、海拔、环境条件等会直接影响 physiology。
assessment 不能只看症状，还要看 exposure context。
持续暴露不切断，病人可能继续恶化。
environmental emergency 常常牵涉温度调节、液体平衡和 perfusion。
keyPointsEn: In environmental emergencies, the cause is often written into the exposure history itself.
Heat, cold, water, altitude, and environmental conditions directly affect physiology.
Assessment must include exposure context, not symptoms alone.
If ongoing exposure is not stopped, the patient may continue to worsen.
Environmental emergencies often involve thermoregulation, fluid balance, and perfusion.
mustKnowZh: 要把 scene history 当成 high-value data。
要先想“病人是否还在暴露中”。
要记住 environment 会直接改变 physiology。
mustKnowEn: Treat scene history as high-value data.
Ask early whether the patient is still being exposed.
Remember that the environment directly changes physiology.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把环境急症当作普通 medical complaint。
常见误区 2：只顾症状，不看 exposure source。
常见误区 3：觉得移离环境是次要动作。实际上这常常是第一步。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating environmental emergencies like ordinary medical complaints.
Common confusion 2: focusing only on symptoms and ignoring the exposure source.
Common confusion 3: thinking removal from the environment is secondary. In reality, it is often step one.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 infectious and communicable diseases，核心不只是感染本身，而是 patient care 与 exposure control 要并行进行。它真正训练的是：如何既照顾病人，又不让现场变成传播点。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers infectious and communicable diseases. Its core is not infection alone, but the need to combine patient care with exposure control. At its heart, it teaches how to care for the patient without turning the scene into a transmission source.
highlightsZh: patient care 和 transmission control 要并行
PPE 和 scene management 很重要
不能只看 fever 一项
highlightsEn: Patient care and transmission control must happen in parallel
PPE and scene management matter greatly
Fever alone is not enough to judge severity
keyPointsZh: 感染类病人 assessment 既包括病情严重度，也包括 exposure risk。
PPE、清洁、隔离和 transport awareness 都是 clinical care 的延伸。
scene management 会影响交叉感染风险。
感染严重度不能只看发烧，还要看整体 appearance、perfusion、respiratory status 和 mentation。
这章把 EMS 的责任从单病人扩展到系统和公共卫生层面。
keyPointsEn: Assessment of infectious patients includes both severity and exposure risk.
PPE, cleaning, isolation, and transport awareness are extensions of clinical care.
Scene management affects cross-transmission risk.
Infectious severity cannot be judged by fever alone; overall appearance, perfusion, respiratory status, and mentation also matter.
This chapter expands EMS responsibility from the individual patient to the system and public-health level.
mustKnowZh: 病人照护和传播控制不能二选一。
PPE 是 clinical logic 的一部分。
scene awareness 也会影响 infection control。
mustKnowEn: Patient care and transmission control are not either/or choices.
PPE is part of clinical logic.
Scene awareness also affects infection control.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：以为 communicable disease 只是 PPE 章节。实际上也包括病情严重度判断。
常见误区 2：只看 fever 判断病情。
常见误区 3：把感染控制看成后勤问题，而不是临床责任。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating communicable disease as only a PPE chapter. It is also about judging illness severity.
Common confusion 2: using fever alone to judge the patient.
Common confusion 3: seeing infection control as logistics rather than clinical responsibility.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 obstetric and gynecologic emergencies，重点在母体风险、胎儿风险、出血、疼痛和 time-sensitive decision-making。它真正训练的是：这类病人不只是“特殊人群”，而是常常有双重风险。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers obstetric and gynecologic emergencies, focusing on maternal risk, fetal risk, bleeding, pain, and time-sensitive decision-making. At its core, it teaches that these are not just “special population” patients, but often dual-risk patients.
highlightsZh: 要同时看母体和潜在胎儿风险
隐私与高风险评估要并行
出血和 shock thinking 很重要
highlightsEn: You must think about maternal and potential fetal risk together
Privacy and high-risk assessment must occur in parallel
Bleeding and shock thinking are very important
keyPointsZh: OB/GYN emergency 常常既需要敏感沟通，也需要高警觉风险判断。
产科问题可能同时涉及两个病人层面的思维：母体与胎儿。
妇科 complaint 也可能迅速演变成 bleeding、shock 或 acute abdomen problem。
这类病人常需要把 privacy、history、severity、transport decision 一起平衡。
不要因为话题敏感就降低 assessment 质量。
keyPointsEn: OB/GYN emergencies often require both sensitive communication and high-alert risk judgment.
Obstetric problems may require thinking about two layers of patients: the mother and the fetus.
Gynecologic complaints may rapidly evolve into bleeding, shock, or acute-abdomen problems.
These patients often require balancing privacy, history, severity, and transport decisions.
Do not let the sensitive nature of the topic reduce assessment quality.
mustKnowZh: 要把 OB/GYN patient 当成 high-risk assessment patient。
要知道 privacy 和 professionalism 很重要，但不能削弱判断。
要把 bleeding risk 和 maternal stability 放在前面。
mustKnowEn: Treat OB/GYN patients as high-risk assessment patients.
Know that privacy and professionalism are important, but must not weaken judgment.
Keep bleeding risk and maternal stability near the top of priorities.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：因为题材敏感而问得不够。
常见误区 2：只把问题当妇科 pain complaint。
常见误区 3：忽略母体稳定度，只顾“是不是怀孕”。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: asking too little because the topic feels sensitive.
Common confusion 2: reducing the case to a gynecologic pain complaint only.
Common confusion 3: focusing only on pregnancy status while ignoring maternal stability.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 neonatal care，重点在出生后最初阶段的新生儿评估和支持。它真正训练的是：新生儿不是小儿版病人，而是一个刚刚完成巨大生理转换的高风险人群。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers neonatal care, focusing on the assessment and support of the newborn during the immediate post-birth period. At its core, it teaches that the neonate is not just a small child, but a high-risk patient undergoing a major physiologic transition.
highlightsZh: 新生儿是独立高风险群体
呼吸、体温、色泽和适应状态都重要
出生后过渡是否顺利是主轴
highlightsEn: Newborns are a distinct high-risk group
Breathing, temperature, color, and adaptation status all matter
The central question is whether post-birth transition is going well
keyPointsZh: 新生儿 care 的核心在于看其是否顺利从宫内环境过渡到宫外环境。
呼吸、体温、反应、色泽和 circulation 都是高优先级观察点。
新生儿 very vulnerable to heat loss，因此温度管理很重要。
这个阶段病人变化可以很快，因此 assessment 与 support 都要及时。
neonatal patient 不是“小儿版本”，其 physiology 和 priority 不同。
keyPointsEn: The core of neonatal care is determining whether the newborn is transitioning successfully from the intrauterine to the extrauterine environment.
Breathing, temperature, responsiveness, color, and circulation are all high-priority observations.
Newborns are very vulnerable to heat loss, so temperature management matters greatly.
Change can occur quickly in this stage, so both assessment and support must be prompt.
A neonatal patient is not simply a smaller pediatric patient; physiology and priorities differ.
mustKnowZh: 要把 newborn 当成独立高风险 patient。
要先看 adaptation 是否顺利。
要特别注意 temperature。
mustKnowEn: Treat the newborn as a distinct high-risk patient.
First ask whether adaptation is occurring successfully.
Pay special attention to temperature.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 neonate 和一般 pediatrics 混为一谈。
常见误区 2：低估 heat loss 的影响。
常见误区 3：觉得只要哭了就一定没问题。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: blending neonatal care into general pediatrics.
Common confusion 2: underestimating the impact of heat loss.
Common confusion 3: assuming that if the baby cries, everything must be fine.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 pediatrics，核心是儿童不是缩小版成人。重点在 appearance、work of breathing、circulation cues、caregiver input 和快速恶化风险。它真正训练的是：儿科 assessment 要更早、更整体地看病人。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers pediatrics, centered on the principle that children are not small adults. It emphasizes appearance, work of breathing, circulation cues, caregiver input, and the risk of rapid deterioration. At its core, it teaches that pediatric assessment must be earlier and more global.
highlightsZh: children are not small adults
general appearance 在儿科价值特别高
孩子可能先代偿后突然恶化
highlightsEn: Children are not small adults
General appearance has especially high value in pediatrics
Children may compensate and then suddenly deteriorate
keyPointsZh: 儿科 assessment 往往先看整体 appearance，而不是一开始就进入细节。
caregiver input 常能提供 baseline 和变化方向。
children may compensate for a while and then crash quickly。
communication、environment 和 caregiver presence 会影响 assessment 质量。
儿科病人很多线索来自 interaction、posture、cry、skin 和 work of breathing。
keyPointsEn: Pediatric assessment often begins with global appearance rather than immediate fine detail.
Caregiver input often provides baseline and direction of change.
Children may compensate for a while and then crash quickly.
Communication, environment, and caregiver presence affect assessment quality.
Many pediatric clues come from interaction, posture, cry, skin signs, and work of breathing.
mustKnowZh: 永远不要把儿童当缩小版成人。
appearance 是 high-value data。
要时刻记得 children can compensate then deteriorate fast。
mustKnowEn: Never treat children as small adults.
Appearance is high-value data.
Always remember that children may compensate and then deteriorate quickly.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：只看数字，不看 appearance。
常见误区 2：觉得孩子还能互动就不危险。
常见误区 3：忽视 caregiver 对 baseline 的价值。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: focusing only on numbers and not on appearance.
Common confusion 2: assuming that because a child still interacts, the situation is not dangerous.
Common confusion 3: overlooking caregiver value in defining baseline.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 geriatrics，重点在老年病人的 baseline、储备能力下降、多病共存和不典型 presentation。它真正训练的是：老年病人不一定“看起来很重”，但风险常常更高。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers geriatrics, focusing on baseline function, reduced physiologic reserve, multimorbidity, and atypical presentation in older adults. At its core, it teaches that older patients may not look dramatic, yet their risk is often higher.
highlightsZh: 老年病人常 presentation 不典型
baseline function 很重要
polypharmacy 和 reduced reserve 会改变判断
highlightsEn: Older adults often present atypically
Baseline function is very important
Polypharmacy and reduced reserve change your interpretation
keyPointsZh: 老年 patient risk 不能只从 complaint 大小判断。
presentation 可能不典型，但 severity 可能更高。
baseline function 和 usual mental status 是解释当前状态的重要前提。
polypharmacy、comorbidity 和 frailty 都会改变病情和反应方式。
老年 assessment 常要求更高的怀疑度，而不是更低。
keyPointsEn: Risk in older patients cannot be judged only by how large the complaint sounds.
Presentation may be atypical while severity is actually higher.
Baseline function and usual mental status are crucial for interpreting the current state.
Polypharmacy, comorbidity, and frailty all change how illness appears and evolves.
Geriatric assessment often demands a higher—not lower—index of suspicion.
mustKnowZh: 不要用年轻成人模板硬套 geriatrics。
baseline 和 polypharmacy 很关键。
“看起来不算夸张”不等于低风险。
mustKnowEn: Do not force a younger-adult template onto geriatrics.
Baseline function and polypharmacy are critical.
“Not dramatic-looking” does not mean low risk.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：觉得 complaint 不重就说明问题小。
常见误区 2：忽略 baseline change。
常见误区 3：低估多病共存和药物相互作用的影响。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: assuming a mild-sounding complaint means a minor problem.
Common confusion 2: ignoring change from baseline.
Common confusion 3: underestimating the impact of multimorbidity and medication interactions.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 patients with special challenges，重点在特殊沟通、行为、发育或功能障碍病人的 assessment 与 interaction。它真正训练的是：不是病人“难搞”，而是你要学会换方法。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers patients with special challenges, focusing on assessment and interaction in people with communication, behavioral, developmental, or functional barriers. At its core, it teaches that the issue is not that the patient is “difficult,” but that you must learn to adapt your approach.
highlightsZh: 沟通方式要跟着病人调整
尊重、安全、耐心最重要
caregiver input 很有价值
highlightsEn: Communication style must be adapted to the patient
Respect, safety, and patience matter most
Caregiver input can be highly valuable
keyPointsZh: 病人有特殊挑战，不代表 assessment 可以放弃。
真正高质量的 assessment 常常来自方法调整，而不是更大声重复。
respect、patience 和 safety 在这章里不是礼貌词，而是策略。
caregiver / family 往往能提供 baseline、triggers 和有效沟通方式。
这章强调个体化 care，而不是一刀切。
keyPointsEn: A patient with special challenges is not a patient who can be left unassessed.
High-quality assessment often comes from adjusting your method, not from repeating yourself more loudly.
Respect, patience, and safety are not polite extras in this chapter—they are strategy.
Caregivers and family often provide baseline information, triggers, and effective communication methods.
This chapter emphasizes individualized care rather than one-size-fits-all care.
mustKnowZh: 要先改方法，不要先贴标签。
要记住沟通失败不等于病人没信息。
要知道 caregiver 可能是 assessment 资源。
mustKnowEn: Adjust your method before you label the patient.
Remember that communication difficulty does not mean the patient has no information.
Know that caregivers may be important assessment resources.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把沟通困难理解成病人不配合。
常见误区 2：觉得这种病人 assessment 不准，所以干脆少做。
常见误区 3：忽略 baseline information 和 trigger pattern。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating communication difficulty as patient noncompliance.
Common confusion 2: assuming assessment will be unreliable and therefore doing less of it.
Common confusion 3: ignoring baseline information and trigger patterns.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 EMS operations，把临床照护放进真实 operational environment 里看，包括 scene、resource、vehicles、lifting 和 safety。它真正训练的是：clinical care 不是在真空中发生的。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers EMS operations by placing clinical care into the real operational environment, including scene factors, resources, vehicles, lifting, and safety. At its core, it teaches that clinical care never happens in a vacuum.
highlightsZh: operations 会直接影响 patient safety
lifting / moving 也是 clinical safety 问题
EMS 不只是“开车送病人”
highlightsEn: Operations directly affect patient safety
Lifting and moving are also clinical safety issues
EMS is not just “driving patients to hospital”
keyPointsZh: EMS operations 不是次要背景，而是 patient outcome 的组成部分。
resource management、scene control 和 movement planning 会改变 care quality。
不安全的搬运和车辆操作同样会伤害病人和 provider。
clinical skill 和 operational skill 在真实工作里是绑在一起的。
这章提醒你：系统、环境和任务执行方式都会影响 clinical success。
keyPointsEn: EMS operations are not secondary background—they are part of patient outcome.
Resource management, scene control, and movement planning affect care quality.
Unsafe lifting or vehicle operation can injure both patient and provider.
Clinical skill and operational skill are tightly linked in real work.
This chapter reminds you that systems, environment, and task execution all influence clinical success.
mustKnowZh: 要把 operations 当成 patient safety 内容。
要知道 lifting / moving 也会改变 outcome。
要理解 EMS 不只是 clinical scene，还有 operational scene。
mustKnowEn: Treat operations as part of patient safety.
Know that lifting and moving can change outcomes.
Understand that EMS is not just a clinical scene, but also an operational scene.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 operations 当成“非临床内容”。
常见误区 2：觉得会看病就够了，环境不重要。
常见误区 3：低估 resource 和 movement plan 的价值。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating operations as “nonclinical content.”
Common confusion 2: thinking that if you are clinically strong, the environment does not matter.
Common confusion 3: underestimating the value of resource planning and movement planning.

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 46 — Hazardous Materials, Incidents, and Response
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 46 — Hazardous Materials, Incidents, and Response
reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 hazardous materials, incidents, and response，核心在 responder safety、hazard recognition、exposure route 和 decontamination。它真正训练的是：别让自己也变成病人。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers hazardous materials, incidents, and response, focusing on responder safety, hazard recognition, exposure route, and decontamination. At its core, it teaches you not to become another patient yourself.
highlightsZh: scene safety 永远优先
hazard recognition 会改变全部策略
decontamination 很关键
highlightsEn: Scene safety always comes first
Hazard recognition changes the whole strategy
Decontamination is critical
keyPointsZh: HazMat scene 和 ordinary medical scene 完全不是同一种逻辑。
危险源可能持续存在，因此 responder safety 是首要条件。
识别物质和暴露方式会改变 PPE、decon 和 treatment strategy。
病人处理不能脱离 scene hazard thinking。
去污是避免二次伤害和 secondary exposure 的关键。
keyPointsEn: A HazMat scene does not follow the same logic as an ordinary medical scene.
The hazard may still be present, so responder safety is the first condition of care.
Identifying the substance and exposure route changes PPE, decontamination, and treatment strategy.
Patient care cannot be separated from scene hazard thinking.
Decontamination is essential to preventing ongoing harm and secondary exposure.
mustKnowZh: 先活下来再救人。
要看 scene 里的 hazard，不只看病人。
decon 不是额外步骤，而是核心步骤之一。
mustKnowEn: Stay safe before trying to rescue.
Look at the hazard in the scene, not only at the patient.
Decontamination is not an extra step; it is one of the core steps.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 HazMat 当普通 medical case。
常见误区 2：只顾快接触病人，不看 hazard。
常见误区 3：觉得去污是后续附属动作。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating HazMat like an ordinary medical case.
Common confusion 2: rushing to the patient without considering the hazard.
Common confusion 3: treating decontamination as a later optional add-on.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 incident management，重点在复杂事件中如何建立组织结构、角色分配和统一控制。它真正训练的是：把混乱变成能管理的流程。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers incident management, focusing on how to build structure, assign roles, and establish unified control in complex events. At its core, it teaches you how to turn chaos into a manageable process.
highlightsZh: large scene 需要 clear roles
incident management 提升效率和安全
它是 operational control，不只是 paperwork
highlightsEn: Large scenes need clear roles
Incident management improves efficiency and safety
It is operational control, not just paperwork
keyPointsZh: 大场景里，没有结构就会快速变乱。
incident management 的核心是角色、沟通、控制和资源协调。
越多人、越多任务，越需要 clear command structure。
好的事件管理会直接影响 patient flow、scene safety 和 treatment efficiency。
这章把系统协作带到比单病人更高的层次。
keyPointsEn: In large scenes, lack of structure quickly becomes chaos.
The core of incident management is role clarity, communication, control, and resource coordination.
The more people and tasks there are, the more a clear command structure matters.
Good incident management directly affects patient flow, scene safety, and treatment efficiency.
This chapter brings system coordination to a level beyond single-patient care.
mustKnowZh: 大场景先想结构，不是先想个人发挥。
role clarity 会直接影响 scene safety。
incident management 是 EMS 能在复杂场景里继续有效工作的关键。
mustKnowEn: In large scenes, think structure before individual improvisation.
Role clarity directly affects scene safety.
Incident management is essential for EMS to function effectively in complex environments.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：觉得 incident management 只是行政流程。
常见误区 2：认为人多就自然会协作。
常见误区 3：低估 role confusion 带来的风险。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating incident management as an administrative exercise.
Common confusion 2: assuming that more people naturally means better teamwork.
Common confusion 3: underestimating the risks created by role confusion.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 multiple-casualty incidents and triage，重点在资源有限时如何为最多病人争取最大总体获益。它真正训练的是：MCI thinking 和 single-patient thinking 不一样。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers multiple-casualty incidents and triage, focusing on how to achieve the greatest overall good when resources are limited. At its core, it teaches that MCI thinking is different from single-patient thinking.
highlightsZh: triage 是动态过程
rapid triage 在 MCI 里价值极高
MCI priority 不等于单病人最佳照护
highlightsEn: Triage is a dynamic process
Rapid triage has enormous value in MCI
MCI priorities are different from optimal single-patient care
keyPointsZh: MCI 的核心是资源有限下的总体优化，而不是单病人完美照护。
rapid triage 的价值在于迅速识别谁最需要有限资源。
triage 不是一次性动作，re-triage 很重要。
MCI 要同时考虑 patient numbers、scene goals 和 resource limits。
这章会改变你对“公平”和“优先级”的直觉。
keyPointsEn: The core of MCI is overall optimization under limited resources, not perfect care for one patient.
Rapid triage matters because it quickly identifies who most needs the available resources.
Triage is not a one-time act; re-triage is essential.
MCI requires thinking about patient numbers, scene goals, and resource limits at the same time.
This chapter changes your instinctive sense of fairness and priority.
mustKnowZh: 要把 MCI 当成系统决策场景。
要知道 triage 是动态的。
要理解最大总体获益和单病人最佳 care 之间的区别。
mustKnowEn: Treat MCI as a systems-decision environment.
Know that triage is dynamic.
Understand the difference between greatest overall good and best single-patient care.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 MCI 还按单病人逻辑处理。
常见误区 2：觉得 triage 一次分完就结束。
常见误区 3：把公平理解成“每个人都给一样资源”。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: trying to manage MCI with single-patient logic.
Common confusion 2: thinking triage is finished after the first pass.
Common confusion 3: defining fairness as giving everyone the same resource.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 air medical transport，重点在航空转运环境与地面 EMS 的差异，包括空间限制、噪音、振动、气压、equipment access 和 coordination。它真正训练的是：空中转运不是“更快的救护车”，而是另一种工作环境。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers air medical transport, focusing on how the air-transport environment differs from ground EMS, including space limits, noise, vibration, pressure changes, equipment access, and coordination. At its core, it teaches that air transport is not just a faster ambulance, but a different work environment altogether.
highlightsZh: air transport 有独立 operational risk
patient packaging 很关键
preparation 和 coordination 价值更高
highlightsEn: Air transport has its own operational risks
Patient packaging is especially important
Preparation and coordination have even greater value
keyPointsZh: 空中环境会改变 assessment、monitoring、movement 和 communication 的难度。
气压、振动、噪音和空间限制都会影响 patient care。
patient packaging 在 air medical scene 中比地面更关键。
air transport 需要更高水平的 planning 和 team coordination。
这章提醒你：transport mode 本身也会改变 care strategy。
keyPointsEn: The air environment changes the difficulty of assessment, monitoring, movement, and communication.
Pressure changes, vibration, noise, and limited space all affect patient care.
Patient packaging becomes even more important in air medical transport than on the ground.
Air transport requires a higher level of planning and team coordination.
This chapter reminds you that transport mode itself can alter care strategy.
mustKnowZh: 要知道 air medical transport 是另一种 clinical environment。
patient packaging 和 preparation 都是高优先级。
transport mode 会改变 risk profile。
mustKnowEn: Know that air medical transport is a different clinical environment.
Patient packaging and preparation are both high priorities.
Transport mode changes the risk profile.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 air transport 当成单纯更快。
常见误区 2：忽略噪音、空间和 access limitations。
常见误区 3：低估 preflight planning 的价值。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating air transport as merely faster.
Common confusion 2: ignoring noise, space, and access limitations.
Common confusion 3: underestimating the value of preflight planning.

reviews.titleZh: Chapter 50 — Vehicle Extrication and Special Rescue
reviews.titleEn: Chapter 50 — Vehicle Extrication and Special Rescue
reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 vehicle extrication and special rescue，重点在危险环境中如何安全接触、解救、转移病人并与技术救援团队协作。它真正训练的是：把救援环境本身当成 patient management 的一部分。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers vehicle extrication and special rescue, focusing on how to safely access, disentangle, and move patients in dangerous environments while coordinating with technical rescue teams. At its core, it teaches you to treat the rescue environment itself as part of patient management.
highlightsZh: extrication 不是单纯快拉出来
scene safety、patient condition、rescue plan 要一起看
多团队协同很关键
highlightsEn: Extrication is not simply pulling the patient out quickly
Scene safety, patient condition, and rescue plan must be considered together
Multi-team coordination is crucial
keyPointsZh: 特殊救援不是普通 scene，它有更高的环境风险。
不安全的 extrication 可能加重原有损伤。
病人 condition 和 rescue environment 需要同时纳入决策。
technical rescue 和 EMS clinical care 必须协同。
这章强调 controlled rescue，而不是 uncontrolled speed。
keyPointsEn: Special rescue scenes carry more environmental risk than ordinary scenes.
Unsafe extrication can worsen existing injury.
The patient’s condition and the rescue environment must both shape decision-making.
Technical rescue and EMS clinical care must be coordinated.
This chapter emphasizes controlled rescue, not uncontrolled speed.
mustKnowZh: 把 extrication 看成高风险 intervention。
要先有 rescue plan，再有 movement。
要知道协作是 special rescue 的核心。
mustKnowEn: Treat extrication as a high-risk intervention.
Have a rescue plan before moving the patient.
Know that coordination is central in special rescue.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：觉得越快越好。
常见误区 2：只看病人，不看环境。
常见误区 3：把技术救援和医疗看成彼此独立。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: assuming faster is always better.
Common confusion 2: focusing only on the patient while ignoring the environment.
Common confusion 3: treating technical rescue and medical care as separate rather than integrated.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 mass gathering medical care，重点在大型活动医疗中的 planning、resource layout、prevention 和群体风险管理。它真正训练的是：活动现场医疗不是随机应变，而是系统预判。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers mass gathering medical care, focusing on planning, resource layout, prevention, and crowd-risk management at large events. At its core, it teaches that event medicine is not random improvisation, but system-level anticipation.
highlightsZh: mass gathering medicine 很依赖 planning
crowd、weather、alcohol/drugs 和 access 都会改变需求
public-health thinking 会进入 EMS scene
highlightsEn: Mass-gathering medicine depends heavily on planning
Crowd size, weather, alcohol/drugs, and access all change demand
Public-health thinking enters the EMS scene
keyPointsZh: 大型活动不只是“病人多一点”，而是资源配置逻辑也改变。
site layout、crowd density、egress、weather 和 substance use 都会影响 patient load。
mass gathering care 很强调预判和前置部署。
这类场景 often combine individual patient care with population risk management。
EMS 在 mass gathering 中会更像系统调度与公共健康参与者。
keyPointsEn: Large events are not simply “more patients”—they also change how resources must be organized.
Site layout, crowd density, egress, weather, and substance use all affect patient load.
Mass-gathering care emphasizes anticipation and advance deployment.
These scenes often combine individual patient care with population-risk management.
In mass gatherings, EMS often acts more like a systems coordinator and public-health participant.
mustKnowZh: 要把 planning 放前面。
要理解 crowd medicine 和普通 EMS response 不一样。
要记住 prevention 也是 event medicine 的一部分。
mustKnowEn: Put planning first.
Understand that crowd medicine differs from ordinary EMS response.
Remember that prevention is part of event medicine.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：觉得活动现场只是把 ambulance 多摆几台。
常见误区 2：忽略 weather 和 crowd flow。
常见误区 3：觉得 event medicine 只看个体病人。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: thinking event care just means adding more ambulances.
Common confusion 2: ignoring weather and crowd flow.
Common confusion 3: treating event medicine as only individual patient care.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 EMS response to terrorism，重点在持续威胁、scene instability、secondary device 风险和 multi-agency coordination。它真正训练的是：在威胁尚未解除时，clinical thinking 必须服从 safety reality。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers EMS response to terrorism, focusing on ongoing threats, scene instability, secondary-device risk, and multi-agency coordination. At its core, it teaches that when threat remains active, clinical thinking must obey safety reality.
highlightsZh: responder safety 绝对优先
terrorism scene 不是普通 MCI
situational awareness 和 coordination 都很关键
highlightsEn: Responder safety is absolutely primary
A terrorism scene is not just an ordinary MCI
Situational awareness and coordination are both crucial
keyPointsZh: terrorism scene 可能有持续威胁，而不是“事件已经结束”。
secondary device、scene instability 和 security risk 会改变全部响应逻辑。
这类事件要求 EMS 与 police / fire / command systems 高度协同。
clinical urgency 不能压过 personal/team safety。
terror response 的核心之一是动态 threat awareness。
keyPointsEn: A terrorism scene may involve an ongoing threat rather than a completed event.
Secondary devices, scene instability, and security risk change the entire response logic.
These incidents require close integration among EMS, police, fire, and command systems.
Clinical urgency cannot override personal and team safety.
A core feature of terror response is dynamic threat awareness.
mustKnowZh: 先安全，再救援。
要知道 terror scene 和 ordinary MCI 不一样。
要持续 reassess threat。
mustKnowEn: Safety first, then rescue.
Know that a terror scene is not the same as an ordinary MCI.
Continuously reassess the threat environment.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：把 terror scene 当成单纯多伤病人场景。
常见误区 2：以为到场第一眼安全就等于一直安全。
常见误区 3：把 multi-agency coordination 看成附属问题。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: treating a terror scene like a standard mass-casualty patient scene.
Common confusion 2: assuming that if the scene looks safe at first, it will remain safe.
Common confusion 3: treating multi-agency coordination as secondary rather than essential.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 disaster management，重点在 planning、logistics、resource coordination 和 recovery。它真正训练的是：灾难不是单个 scene，而是系统级长期管理问题。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers disaster management, focusing on planning, logistics, resource coordination, and recovery. At its core, it teaches that disasters are not just single scenes, but long-duration system-level management problems.
highlightsZh: disaster management 是系统视角
logistics 很关键
recovery 也是灾难管理的一部分
highlightsEn: Disaster management requires a systems perspective
Logistics matter enormously
Recovery is also part of disaster management
keyPointsZh: 灾难管理不仅是现场响应，也包括资源延续、组织韧性和恢复过程。
planning 和 logistics 会决定系统能否撑住长时间响应。
disaster scene 往往超出单一单位能力，需要区域和系统层级思维。
recovery 不是附录，而是 disaster management 的一部分。
这章把 EMS 从单病人、单事件思维推向 system resilience。
keyPointsEn: Disaster management includes not only scene response but also sustained resources, organizational resilience, and recovery.
Planning and logistics determine whether the system can sustain prolonged response.
Disaster scenes often exceed the capacity of a single unit and require regional/system-level thinking.
Recovery is not an appendix; it is part of disaster management itself.
This chapter pushes EMS from single-patient or single-scene thinking toward system resilience.
mustKnowZh: 灾难不是普通 MCI 放大版，而是系统问题。
logistics 决定系统能不能撑住。
recovery 也要纳入管理范围。
mustKnowEn: A disaster is not just a larger MCI—it is a systems problem.
Logistics determine whether the system can sustain itself.
Recovery must also be included in management thinking.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：觉得灾难管理只看初始响应。
常见误区 2：把 logistics 当成后勤，不当成核心。
常见误区 3：忽略 recovery phase。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: thinking disaster management is only about the initial response.
Common confusion 2: treating logistics as support-only rather than central.
Common confusion 3: ignoring the recovery phase.

reviews.summaryZh: 这章讲 crime scene awareness，重点在 responder safety、evidence preservation 和在不妨碍救命 care 的前提下保护现场。它真正训练的是：你不能只看到病人，还要看到病人所在的场。
reviews.summaryEn: This chapter covers crime scene awareness, focusing on responder safety, evidence preservation, and protecting the scene without interfering with lifesaving care. At its core, it teaches that you must see not only the patient, but also the scene the patient belongs to.
highlightsZh: patient care 和 scene preservation 要平衡
EMS 会影响 evidence integrity
documentation 对后续调查也很重要
highlightsEn: Patient care and scene preservation must be balanced
EMS actions affect evidence integrity
Documentation matters for later investigation as well
keyPointsZh: crime scene awareness 不是为了妨碍救人，而是为了减少不必要破坏。
EMS 往往是最早进入现场的单位之一，因此行为会影响安全和证据。
scene safety 仍然是第一位。
documentation 不只是临床记录，也可能影响后续法律和调查。
这章把 EMS 的视角从单病人治疗扩展到现场整体。
keyPointsEn: Crime-scene awareness is not about delaying care, but about avoiding unnecessary disruption.
EMS is often among the earliest agencies on scene, so its actions affect both safety and evidence.
Scene safety remains first priority.
Documentation is not only clinical—it may also matter for later legal or investigative processes.
This chapter expands EMS perspective from treating one patient to managing the whole scene context.
mustKnowZh: 要在 care 和 preservation 之间找平衡。
要知道 EMS 行为会影响 evidence。
要把 scene awareness 看成 provider safety 的一部分。
mustKnowEn: Balance care with preservation.
Know that EMS actions can affect evidence.
Treat scene awareness as part of provider safety.
commonConfusionsZh: 常见误区 1：觉得 crime scene awareness 会妨碍 patient care。
常见误区 2：以为 evidence preservation 只关警察。
常见误区 3：忽略 documentation 对 investigation 的价值。
commonConfusionsEn: Common confusion 1: thinking crime-scene awareness interferes with patient care.
Common confusion 2: assuming evidence preservation matters only to police.
Common confusion 3: overlooking the value of documentation in investigation.
